w:r.w:t: Kim targets boutique fitness studio owners on the South Shore of MA. Competitors are primarily studio owners doing marketing themselves — not agencies. The value proposition is replacing poor DIY execution with 10+ years of professional growth marketing, website management, and marketing operations experience.
Phase 1 — Intro rates (first 2-3 paying clients): Lower end of ranges while building portfolio.
Phase 2 — Standard rates (after documented results): Full rates after 2-3 clients with measurable outcomes. Existing clients get 60 days notice.
1-2 website builds free to build portfolio. Cap at two. Require written testimonial, before/after metrics, and a signed agreement allowing case study use.
Important: Be Well Studios and similar ICP-quality prospects are NOT free clients. Save free builds for lower-stakes relationships.

Website, Google Business Profile, SEO/AEO metadata, booking/lead flow, and existing email setup reviewed. Delivered as a written PDF report with prioritized findings and recommended next steps within 5 business days of intake form completion.
Not included: social media audit, paid advertising, print
The audit deliverable IS the proposal. The last section includes a proposed site map, timeline, and investment summary. The contract and 50% deposit conversation happens at audit delivery.

For studios that need to define what they are saying before a website can be built. Includes: brand positioning, core messaging (tagline, value proposition, service descriptions), about us narrative, content hierarchy, and a page-by-page copy brief.
Not included: logo design, visual identity, photography. Kim refers to a graphic designer in her network — client hires the designer directly.
Up to 8 pages. Additional pages at $300/page. Complex integrations quoted separately.
Built in client's existing CMS where possible; Squarespace for net-new clients
Booking/registration integration, contact forms, lead capture
On-page SEO/AEO setup (titles, meta, image alt text, sitemap, schema)
Custom elements via Claude Code HTML export where needed
Full team training + written documentation handoff
Client provides: all copy, logo files (SVG + PNG), brand colors (hex), fonts, photography.
Payment: 50% deposit at kickoff, 50% at launch.
Kill fee: If project is paused 30+ days due to client inaction, it is considered abandoned. Deposit is non-refundable. Restarting requires a new deposit.
For new builds: decided in discovery call, scoped alongside the website, built in from day one. For existing sites: standalone add-on available anytime.
Website forms connected to email platform
List and segment structure (lightweight CRM - e.g., Pilates list, CrossFit list)
Full team training on how to clone, build, send, and manage lists
Platform: Kim works within client's existing platform. Brevo is the default for new clients.
For clients who have email infrastructure but need fresh templates. Includes 3 branded templates (newsletter, promotional, general) + training. Additional templates beyond 3 priced per project.
For clients who have a functioning website and team but need a marketing brain — not execution, not maintenance, just direction. A true consulting partnership.
Recommendations delivered in writing after each session
Client’s team executes; Kim directs. No maintenance or content work included. Natural entry point for clients not ready for a full retainer — and a path to Ops Partner.
Scope-based, not hours-based. Internal guideline: if a client consistently requires more than ~4 hours/month, the upgrade conversation to Ops Partner begins.
Minor content updates: text, images, class schedules, link fixes
Employee onboarding to website (launch session only)
Not included: new pages or sections, structural design changes, new features, email management. Upgrade trigger: client regularly needs new sections or pages -> Ops Partner conversation.
Includes everything in Care Plan, plus:
Monthly 30-minute strategy call (Kim drives with prepared report)
Lead workflow setup and optimization: CTA definition, sign-up flow, post-lead experience
Email template assistance: Kim guides, client executes
Welcome sequence updates: Kim assists and guides

Not included: new pages, new features, email campaign management (without add-on), social, paid ads, copywriting, photography, print. Upgrade trigger: client needs new pages, blog, or wants Kim to own email -> Full Partner conversation.
Includes everything in Ops Partner, plus:
Blog management: topic suggestions, publishing, SEO/AEO optimization per post
Writing assistance (Kim assists with structure; client provides final voice and approval)
Email campaign management: builds, schedules, sends (requires Email Marketing Setup first)
Deeper monthly reporting: website + email + lead performance combined
Not included: social media management, paid ads, copywriting, photography, print.
Has a working website. Needs it maintained and reported on. Low-touch, predictable. Typically a post-build client who does not need ongoing strategic input.
Foundation is in place. Needs someone to own the infrastructure, optimize lead flow, and bring strategic thinking monthly. Can execute on their own when given direction. Example: Be Well Studios.

Wants Kim embedded. Busier, more complex, or growing fast. Wants content strategy, blog management, and email handled. May have multiple service lines or an owner without bandwidth for content. Buying a marketing brain, not just maintenance.
Shorthand: Ops Partner clients execute. Full Partner clients hand it off.
Client has platform in place, needs templates refreshed
Client needs full infrastructure built from scratch
Client wants Kim to manage and send campaigns
Full email management included in retainer
How email integrates with the website: Website forms -> email platform lists -> list/segment structure acts as lightweight CRM. Set up once during Email Marketing Setup, trained on at handoff.
— 60 min video. Kim drives agenda. Granola captures notes.
— Written. Client signs off before anything starts.
— Timeline does not start until payment clears.
— See Section 8. Kim helps set up anything the client does not have.
— Full picture of existing site before any concepts are presented.
— Built in Lovable from discovery notes + crawl findings. Presented for approval before build begins.
— Staging link shared. All feedback batched in one round.
— Final changes only. No new scope.
— Full team trained. Written docs delivered.
Kill fee: If project is paused 30+ days due to client inaction, it is considered abandoned. Deposit is non-refundable. Restarting requires a new deposit.
Collected in full at intake. If a client does not have something, Kim either skips it or sets it up. Nothing starts until this checklist is complete.
Logo files: SVG and PNG minimum
Any existing content to carry over
Technical Access (never via email or text)
Domain registrar login — to update DNS records (different from website builder login)
Existing website CMS login — for content reference and migration
Google Analytics 4 — admin preferred, read acceptable
Google Search Console — admin preferred; Kim sets up if they do not have it
Booking software admin: Mindbody, Vagaro, Acuity, Pike13, etc.
Security rule: Credentials are never shared over email or text. A password manager or secure sharing tool is used for all transfers.
— Goals, brand vision, how visitors should feel, what success looks like. Must happen before any audit work begins.
— GA4 (admin preferred) + Search Console (Kim sets up if not present) + current website login.
— Full site inventory: pages, response codes, broken links, page structure. Combined with GA4 shows what exists and how it is performing.
— Repeatable template, simple language, sectioned by category. Each finding: what is happening -> what I would do -> what it means for your business.
— PDF sent within 5 business days. Present on video or in person if the vibe is right.
— Audit delivery IS the close. Report ends with proposed site map, timeline, and investment. Contract conversation flows from the findings.
Client name, date, Waveform Marketing. One sentence capturing the single biggest opportunity.
Tools used (Screaming Frog, GA4, Search Console, manual review). Establishes credibility.
Plain-language summary: pages, what is working, what is broken, what is missing. No jargon.
Section 3 - How You Show Up Online
SEO/AEO health from the customer perspective. Keywords, rankings, metadata, GBP, Search Console, AI search visibility.
CTAs, booking flow, forms, post-submission experience. Walk the full lead journey from first click to member.
List status, automations, post-sign-up experience. Gaps flagged with Email Marketing Setup as solution.
High / Medium / Low priority. Each finding: what is happening -> what I would do -> what it means for your business.
Proposed site map. Pages to migrate, cut, add - with one-line rationale tied to discovery call goals.
Section 8 - What This Means for Your Business
Outcomes tied to what the client said they wanted. Concrete and business-specific, not generic SEO language.
Visual project timeline scaled to site complexity. Investment summary. Clear next step: sign, deposit, set kickoff date.
Default. Business plan minimum required for custom code injection.
Stay where they are: WordPress, Wix, Squarespace
Custom-built with no CMS, or too outdated
Bespoke components embedded into any CMS
Brevo as default for new/no-platform clients
Client owns accounts; Kim has admin access
Client is on WordPress, Wix, or Squarespace
Client is on something custom or unworkable

Client always owns and pays for their own accounts. Kim is added as admin contributor. If the relationship ends, everything stays with the client and Kim removes herself. Kim never pays for client subscriptions.
Social media management (by design: "I work alongside your social person and handle everything they can't")
Paid advertising (Google Ads, Meta Ads, etc.)
Logo and visual identity design (refer to network designer)
Out-of-scope requests: Kim refers clients to agency partners. Referral relationships to formalize: (1) graphic designer for logo/visual identity, (2) social media agency serving fitness studios.
Scenario A: 2 Ops Partner + 1 Care Plan
Scenario B: 1 Full Partner + 1 Ops Partner + 1 Care Plan

3 clients at target revenue is roughly one full work day per week - fits inside a 9-2 schedule. Heavier lift is upfront (builds, email setup). Once on retainer, very manageable alongside a corporate job.
Brand & Messaging ($1,500) -> Designer referral -> Website Build ($3,500) -> Email Setup ($1,800) -> Retainer
Audit ($750) -> Website Build + Email Setup ($5,300) -> Retainer
Audit ($750) -> Contract signed -> Website Build ($3,500) -> Retainer
Retainer contract terms: notice period, minimum commitment length, pause policy ✓ RESOLVED — see Section 16
Proposal structure: what Kim actually sends a prospect to close a deal
Phase 2 pricing triggers: specific milestone that moves from intro to standard rates
Referral partner formalization: graphic designer + social media agency
Reporting templates: what the monthly SEO/AEO report actually looks like
Consulting schedule structure alongside dance classes
Terms That Apply to All Retainers
3-month minimum commitment on all retainers (Strategy Advisory, Care Plan, Ops Partner, Full Partner)
Billed at the beginning of each month
30-day written notice to cancel after the 3-month minimum is met
No pause policy — cancel or continue. No exceptions.
Contract references the retainer comparison chart directly
60-day written notice required for any price increases. Existing clients move to Phase 2 rates with 60-day notice after documented results.
Retainer is optional after a website build. Clients may walk away with full ownership of their site. No pressure — some clients will self-manage and refer.
All website files, content, and digital assets created during the engagement are owned by the client upon receipt of final payment.
Waveform Marketing retains no rights to client assets after the engagement ends.

Client owns and maintains all accounts: domain registrar, CMS, Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Google Business Profile, and all marketing platforms. Kim is added as administrator during the engagement and removes herself upon termination.
Waveform Marketing is never listed as account owner on any client platform. Kim never pays for client subscriptions.
All credentials shared via a secure password manager (e.g., 1Password or Bitwarden). Never via email or text.
Scope creep: how to handle and document out-of-scope requests in writing
Waveform Marketing | kim@waveformmarketing.com | waveformmarketing.com | Confidential - June 2026
